generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance GantryPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Encoder sensor measuring joint angles of the gantry crane's horizontal and vertical axes for precise position tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GantryPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance PayloadForceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Force/torque sensor measuring payload interaction forces and stability metrics during crane operation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PayloadForceDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor monitoring actuator motor currents to detect operational anomalies or energy consumption patterns."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance CraneTrajectorySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor system capturing trajectory execution metrics including timestamps, waypoints, and residual motion measurements."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TrajectoryExecutionDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generic sensor capturing laboratory environmental conditions (temperature, humidity) affecting crane operation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance GantryPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GantryPositionEncoder
]

instance PayloadForceDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PayloadForceSensor
]

instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance TrajectoryExecutionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneTrajectorySensor
]

instance EnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based kinematic model of the gantry crane generating forward/inverse kinematic trajectories for payload movement."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KinematicEngineEnabler
]

instance DynamicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic model simulating crane payload interactions including forces, torques, and energy consumption under various operational conditions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DynamicSimulationEnabler
]

instance TrajectoryOptimizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Optimization model generating collision-free, energy-efficient paths using CPLEX/Gurobi solvers with velocity and payload stability constraints."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrajectoryOptimizationEnabler
]

instance CraneStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data representing real-time crane state including position, velocity, and acceleration of gantry, puck, and payload."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GantryPositionDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBEnabler
]

instance ControllerTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Controller telemetry including motor currents, encoder readings, and sensor feedback for real-time system monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted, GantryPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FaultDetectionAlgorithmEnabler, HistoricalDataLoggingService
]

instance TrajectoryExecutionMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Detailed trajectory execution metrics including timestamps, waypoints, and residual motion measurements for validation purposes."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TrajectoryExecutionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContinuousValidationFrameworkEnabler, GrafanaDashboardEnabler
]

instance HistoricalOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Archived operational metrics including actuator positions, forces, and energy consumption for post-mortem analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneStateData, ControllerTelemetryData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HistoricalDataLoggingService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance KinematicEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation engine (e.g., PyBullet) implementing forward/inverse kinematics for crane motion planning."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService
]

instance DynamicSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dynamic simulation platform (e.g., MATLAB/Simulink) modeling payload interactions and energy consumption."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService
]

instance TrajectoryOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization solver (e.g., CPLEX) generating energy-efficient, collision-free trajectories with constraint enforcement."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService
]

instance TimescaleDBEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing high-resolution crane state data with timestamp indexing for real-time and historical queries."
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataLoggingService
]

instance MQTTBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT broker facilitating event-driven communication between services (e.g., trajectory validation alerts)."
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
]

instance GrafanaDashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/2D visualization dashboard rendering CAD models overlaid with real-time sensor data for validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance FaultDetectionAlgorithmEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine learning/rule-based algorithms (e.g., Python scripts) detecting faults via torque spikes or position errors."
    DTDFVocab:enables FaultDetectionService
]

instance ContinuousValidationFrameworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time validation framework comparing digital twin predictions with physical telemetry using statistical methods."
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
]

instance AutomationMLModelEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AutomationML-based structured representation of physical and logical crane components for standardized modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemEvolutionService
]

// C6: Services
instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time trajectory planning and motion optimization generating collision-free, energy-efficient paths using kinematic/dynamic models."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalTrajectoryInsight, ControllerConstraintAction
]

instance HistoricalDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Time-series data storage and retrieval logging operational metrics via bidirectional streaming for performance benchmarking."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalMetricsInsight, FaultDiagnosisAction
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/2D dashboards rendering CAD models with real-time sensor data for physical/digital state comparison."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateVisualizationInsight, ValidationComparisonAction
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model-plant synchronization implementing real-time validation methods to detect discrepancies and trigger corrections."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelPlantConsistencyInsight, CorrectiveActionTrigger
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection system identifying faults (e.g., motor stalls) via machine learning analysis of data stream deviations."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultIdentificationInsight, MaintenanceAlertAction
]

instance SystemEvolutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Metadata-driven system evolution notation (DarTwin) documenting and version-controlling digital twin changes with lifecycle traceability."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemChangeDocumentationInsight, ModelUpdateAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OptimalTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Generated collision-free, energy-efficient trajectory paths optimized for crane payload transport."
]

instance ControllerConstraintAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enforces velocity limits and payload stability constraints through controller integration."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OperationalMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical operational metrics including actuator positions, forces, and energy consumption for performance analysis."
]

instance FaultDiagnosisAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Post-mortem analysis of operational data to identify performance issues or system faults."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SystemStateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D/2D visualization of crane CAD models overlaid with sensor data for state comparison."
]

instance ValidationComparisonAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Comparative analysis between physical and digital twin states to validate synchronization accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ModelPlantConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time comparison results indicating discrepancies between digital twin predictions and physical telemetry."
]

instance CorrectiveActionTrigger : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated trigger for corrective actions when validation discrepancies exceed thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FaultIdentificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected anomalies including motor stalls, sensor failures, or unexpected torque spikes in crane operation."
]

instance MaintenanceAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Alerts predictive maintenance teams about identified faults requiring immediate attention."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SystemChangeDocumentationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Documented changes in digital twin models, services, or configurations with traceability to physical system lifecycle."
]

instance ModelUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Version-controlled updates to digital twin models based on system evolution requirements."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance CraneActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanically actuated components (motors, encoders) executing motion commands and enforcing operational constraints."
]

instance ControllerHardware : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Dedicated hardware/software system interfacing with crane actuators to execute motion commands and enforce constraints."
]

// C1: Environment
instance LabScaleGantryCraneEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Controlled laboratory environment with predefined workspace boundaries, payload constraints, and safety protocols."
    base:contains CraneActuator, ControllerHardware, GantryPositionEncoder,
                 PayloadForceSensor, MotorCurrentSensor, CraneTrajectorySensor,
                 EnvironmentalSensor
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in the Digital Twin system is mediated by the **Control Application** and **Trajectory Generator Service**, which translate digital commands into physical actions for the **Physical Crane**. Key aspects include:

**Command Types**:
- **Trajectory commands**: Generated by the **Trajectory Generator Service** using a kinematics model to produce optimal movement paths (e.g., spline-based trajectories with zero residual swinging).
- **Manual overrides**: Issued via the **Human Machine Interface** (e.g., emergency stops, ad-hoc repositioning).

**Control Message Formats**:
- **Structured payloads** (likely JSON or protobuf) containing:
  - Target coordinates (X, Y, Z).
  - Velocity/acceleration profiles.
  - Safety thresholds (e.g., maximum load, collision avoidance zones).

**Communication Protocol**:
- Commands are transmitted from the **Control Application** to the **Physical Crane Interface** via an unspecified protocol (likely TCP/IP or serial), which then interfaces with the crane’s controllers.

**Validation and Authentication**:
- **Trajectory Validation**: The **Continuous Validation Service** compares executed trajectories (from the **Logging Application**) with simulated trajectories (from the **Simulation Service**) using distance metrics. If deviations exceed thresholds, alerts are triggered via the **Dashboard**.
- **Authentication**: Not explicitly mentioned, but implied for operator-authorized commands (e.g., via **Human Machine Interface** session management).

**Feedback Systems**:
- **Closed-loop control**: The **Physical Crane Interface** provides real-time feedback (e.g., encoder data, force sensors) to the **Control Application** for adaptive adjustments.
- **Conflict Resolution**: Handled by the **Control Application**, which prioritizes commands based on:
  - **Trajectory priority** (e.g., preempting manual overrides for safety-critical paths).
  - **Threshold breaches** (e.g., halting motion if load exceeds capacity).

**Command Execution Confirmation**:
- **Acknowledgment signals** are sent back to the **Control Application** via the **Physical Crane Interface**, confirming receipt and execution status. Failures (e.g., hardware errors) trigger alerts in the **Dashboard**.
"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems in the documents follow a **lightweight, ad-hoc orchestration pattern** without reliance on commercial frameworks. The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar employs a **message-driven architecture** using **ActiveMQ** for inter-component communication, paired with an **InfluxDB database** for time-series data storage, indicating a **publisher-subscriber model** for behavioral adaptation. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar uses **Microsoft Azure tools** for pipeline implementation, leveraging **AutomationML** for model-driven engineering, suggesting a **hybrid orchestration** of **simulation, prediction, and runtime visualization** services. The **gantry crane** system (described via **C4 Model**) implies a **containerized microservices approach**, though specifics on **resource management** (e.g., Kubernetes, auto-scaling) or **system-wide coordination** (e.g., event brokers, service meshes) are absent. All exemplars avoid **monolithic frameworks**, opting instead for **modular, technology-agnostic integration** (e.g., Raspberry Pi + sensors, VMs, Azure cloud services)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar emphasizes **self-adaptive behavioral models** for both the physical greenhouse and its digital twin, suggesting dynamic fidelity adjustments to maintain alignment between the two. Validation and verification methods are not explicitly described, but the use of **prerecorded data** in virtual machines implies a reliance on historical or synthetic datasets for model calibration. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar focuses on **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction**, indicating iterative validation through real-time comparisons between digital twin outputs and physical sensor measurements. Uncertainty sources (e.g., sensor noise, model approximations) are not quantified, nor are error detection/correction mechanisms detailed. Quality assurance processes are inferred to involve **AutomationML-based standardization** and **Microsoft Azure tools** for pipeline consistency, though no formal validation protocols (e.g., cross-validation, benchmarking) are mentioned. The ad-hoc nature of the implementations (per the discussion) suggests limited formal fidelity assessment frameworks."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s Technical Connection** infrastructure is designed for low-latency, high-reliability communication between services and the physical system. Key components include:

**Communication Protocols**:
- **MQTT**: Used for lightweight, event-driven signaling between services (e.g., trajectory validation alerts, operator notifications).
- **Database Protocols**: **TimescaleDB** supports PostgreSQL-compatible queries for time-series data, with optimized read/write operations.
- **Service-to-Service**: Likely HTTP/REST or gRPC for inter-container communication (e.g., **Trajectory Generator Service** ↔ **Simulation Service**).

**Network Topology and Architecture**:
- **Star topology**: Services communicate via the **MQTT Broker** as a central hub, with the **Database** acting as a shared data layer.
- **Hybrid deployment**: The **Physical Crane Interface** and **Control Application** operate locally (edge), while the **Digital Twin services** (e.g., **Simulation Service**, **Dashboard**) may reside on a local server or cloud (not explicitly specified).

**Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
- **High-bandwidth**: Required for real-time trajectory data streaming (e.g., >100 Hz for precise motion control).
- **Low-latency**: Critical for closed-loop control (e.g., <50 ms round-trip delay between **Control Application** and **Physical Crane Interface**).

**Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
- **Database Replication**: **TimescaleDB** supports high availability via replication.
- **Service Resilience**: The **Continuous Validation Service** includes threshold-based fail-safes (e.g., alerting operators for trajectory deviations).
- **Fallback Modes**: The **Physical Crane** can operate independently of the Digital Twin if network issues arise.

**Security Protocols**:
- **Not explicitly detailed**, but implied measures include:
  - **Authentication**: For operator access via the **Human Machine Interface**.
  - **Data Encryption**: For sensitive trajectory/command data in transit (e.g., TLS for MQTT/HTTP).
  - **Access Control**: Role-based permissions (e.g., researchers vs. operators).
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is structured to support real-time operation, scalability, and research flexibility. Key details include:

**Hosting Platforms**:
- **Edge Deployment**: The **Physical Crane Interface**, **Control Application**, and **Logging Application** run locally on the crane’s embedded system or a nearby edge server to minimize latency.
- **On-Premise/Cloud Hybrid**: The **Digital Twin services** (e.g., **Database**, **Dashboard**, **Trajectory Generator Service**) are likely hosted on a local server or cloud platform (e.g., Kubernetes for container orchestration), enabling:
  - **Scalability**: Horizontal scaling of services (e.g., additional **Simulation Service** instances for parallel trajectory testing).
  - **Resource Isolation**: Separation of physical control logic (edge) from virtual analysis (cloud/on-premise).

**Computational and Storage Requirements**:
- **CPU/GPU**: The **Trajectory Generator Service** and **Simulation Service** require computational resources for kinematic modeling and physics simulations (e.g., real-time trajectory optimization).
- **Storage**: **TimescaleDB** stores time-series data with compression for historical analysis, while the **Dashboard** (Grafana) caches visualizations for low-latency rendering.
- **Memory**: In-memory caching for frequently accessed trajectory data (e.g., Redis or TimescaleDB’s built-in caching).

**Scalability Mechanisms**:
- **Containerization**: Services are deployed as independent containers (e.g., Docker), allowing dynamic scaling (e.g., adding **Simulation Service** instances during peak research loads).
- **Database Partitioning**: **TimescaleDB** supports hypertable partitioning for large-scale data ingestion.

**Deployment Models Supported**:
- **Monolithic for Physical Control**: The **Control Application** and **Physical Crane Interface** are tightly coupled for deterministic real-time operation.
- **Microservices for Digital Twin**: Services like **Trajectory Generator**, **Simulation**, and **Validation** are modular, enabling independent updates and scaling.

**Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
- **High Availability**: **TimescaleDB** replication and service redundancy (e.g., MQTT broker failover).
- **Fault Tolerance**: The **Physical Crane** includes hardware-level redundancies (e.g., backup controllers), while the Digital Twin services implement:
  - **Automatic retries** for failed commands (e.g., via the **Control Application**).
  - **Graceful degradation** (e.g., switching to manual control if the Digital Twin fails).
- **Monitoring**: The **Dashboard** (Grafana) provides real-time health metrics for all services.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems demonstrate **limited but explicit horizontal integration** with external systems. The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar relies on **InfluxDB** (time-series database) and **ActiveMQ** (message broker), which are **standardized but not proprietary**, enabling interoperability with other IoT/edge systems. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar integrates **AutomationML** (an **ISO/IEC 62714-compliant** standard for engineering data exchange) and **Microsoft Azure tools**, suggesting **cloud-native interoperability** with enterprise systems (e.g., ERP, SCADA) via **Azure’s PaaS offerings**. The **gantry crane** system lacks explicit details, but its **C4 Model architecture** implies potential integration with **logistics/CPS ecosystems** (e.g., container tracking, warehouse management). No mention of **standardized APIs** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT), **federated DTs**, or **enterprise system dependencies** (e.g., SAP, MES) is provided beyond the Azure/AutomationML stack in the Air Quality case."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin exemplars exhibit **partial standardization** with domain-specific and technology-centric approaches:
  - **Air Quality Management**: Adopts **AutomationML** for digital twin modeling, aligning with **construction/building automation standards** (e.g., ISO 12231 for HVAC systems). Microsoft Azure tools imply compliance with **cloud interoperability standards** (e.g., OData, REST APIs).
  - **GreenhouseDT**: Uses **InfluxDB** (time-series database standard) and **ActiveMQ** (JMS/AMQP protocols), but no explicit agricultural or IoT standards (e.g., OMA LWM2M, ISA-95) are mentioned.
  - **Gantry Crane**: No standards are referenced; the **C4 Model** is a software architecture notation, not an interoperability framework. The exemplar’s cyber-physical focus suggests potential alignment with **logistics standards** (e.g., EPCglobal, W3C SSN Ontology) if extended, but this is speculative.
  Certification requirements or compliance frameworks (e.g., GDPR, IEC 62443) are **not in Document**. Interoperability is ad-hoc, relying on proprietary toolchains (e.g., Azure, AutomationML) rather than open standards like **Digital Twin Management Interface (DTMI)** or **FIWARE**."
]
